metadata.title: Supplementary Cementitious Materials (SCM) Market Overview
metadata.description: Comprehensive overview of the US and global SCM market including fly ash, slag cement, natural pozzolans, calcined clay, silica fume, and emerging alternatives
metadata.notes: SCMs are materials that, when combined with Portland cement, contribute to concrete properties through hydraulic or pozzolanic activity. SCM usage reduces cement content, lowers cost, improves durability, and reduces carbon footprint.

key_growth_drivers: Carbon emission reduction mandates (cement sector responsible for ~8% of global CO2)
Declining fresh fly ash availability driving alternative SCM development

us_market.market_structure: Fragmented with mix of utility byproduct marketers, cement companies, and specialty suppliers

segments.name: fly_ash
segments.full_name: Coal Fly Ash (Class C and Class F)
segments.astm_standard: ASTM C618 - Coal Fly Ash and Raw or Calcined Natural Pozzolan for Use in Concrete
segments.supply_trend_detail: Fresh fly ash production declining 5-8% annually as coal plants retire. Harvested/reclaimed ash from ponds partially offsetting but quality can be variable.
segments.price_trend: RISING - 8-12% annual price increases as supply tightens

classes.geographic_concentration: Central/Western US (PRB coal belt - WY, MT, TX lignite)

quality_concerns: Harvested/reclaimed ash may have higher LOI (loss on ignition) and carbon content
Variability between fresh and harvested sources

segments.name: slag_cement
segments.astm_standard: ASTM C989 - Slag Cement for Use in Concrete and Mortars
segments.supply_trend_detail: US blast furnace capacity declining as EAF steelmaking grows. Cleveland-Cliffs and USS shifting toward DRI/EAF. Import dependence increasing.

segments.name: natural_pozzolans
segments.full_name: Natural Pozzolans (Volcanic Ash, Pumice, Diatomaceous Earth, Calcined Shale)
segments.astm_standard: ASTM C618 Class N - Raw or Calcined Natural Pozzolan
segments.supply_trend_detail: Increasing interest as fly ash substitute. Volcanic deposits in Western US (OR, CA, AZ, NM, ID) being developed. Supply is geologically abundant but processing/logistics costs limit distribution.

typical_replacement_rates.notes: Similar replacement levels to Class F fly ash. Performance varies by source.

advantages: Not dependent on fossil fuel industry
Available in Western US where fly ash is scarce
challenges: High transportation costs from remote deposits
Limited DOT/specification approval in some states

segments.name: calcined_clay
segments.astm_standard: ASTM C618 Class N (natural pozzolan); ASTM C1697 (blended cement with calcined clay)
segments.supply_trend_detail: LC3 (Limestone Calcined Clay Cement) technology being commercialized globally. US production in early stages. Significant investment by Holcim, Heidelberg, and others.
segments.price_trend: Expected to decline as scale increases

lc3_technology.description: Limestone Calcined Clay Cement - combines clinker (50%), calcined clay (30%), limestone (15%), and gypsum (5%)
lc3_technology.energy_requirement: Lower than clinker production (no limestone decarbonation)
lc3_technology.global_status: Commercial in India, Colombia, Cuba; pilot in US, Europe
lc3_technology.us_status: Pilot/demonstration phase. Holcim conducting trials at multiple US plants.

challenges: Competing with established fly ash and slag

segments.name: silica_fume
segments.astm_standard: ASTM C1240 - Silica Fume Used in Cementitious Mixtures
segments.supply_trend_detail: Byproduct of silicon/ferrosilicon production. Limited growth in US production. Imports supplement.
segments.price_trend: STABLE - premium product with stable niche

segments.name: ground_glass_pozzolan
segments.full_name: Ground Glass Pozzolan (GGP / Finely Ground Recycled Glass)
segments.astm_standard: ASTM C1866 - Ground Glass Pozzolan for Use in Concrete
segments.supply_trend_detail: Emerging SCM from recycled container glass. New ASTM C1866 standard (2020) enabling specification acceptance. Multiple processing facilities starting up.

typical_replacement_rates.notes: Performance similar to Class F fly ash

challenges: Contamination control from mixed glass streams
Limited track record vs fly ash/slag
Specification acceptance still expanding across DOTs
Competing with low-cost fly ash (where available)

segments.name: limestone_filler
segments.full_name: Ground Limestone (Limestone Powder / Calcium Carbonate Filler)
segments.astm_standard: ASTM C595 (component of Type IL cement); ASTM C1157 (performance-based)
segments.supply_trend_detail: Portland-limestone cement (PLC/Type IL) is fastest-growing cement type. Limestone replaces 5-15% of clinker at the cement plant. Every major US cement producer now offers Type IL.
segments.price_trend: STABLE - limestone is very low cost

key_players.role: Incorporated in Type IL cement at the plant

typical_replacement_rates.notes: Interground with clinker at the cement plant, not added at the concrete plant

advantages: Abundantly available (limestone is cement raw material)
Reduces clinker factor and CO2 by 8-12%
No additional infrastructure needed (interground at cement plant)
Faster specification acceptance than other alternatives
segments.notes: Type IL is not technically an SCM addition at the concrete plant - it is a blended cement produced at the cement plant. But it serves the same market function of reducing Portland cement clinker content.

astm_standards_summary.title: Portland Cement
astm_standards_summary.types: Type I, II, III, IV, V

astm_standards_summary.title: Hydraulic Cement (Performance Specification)
astm_standards_summary.types: GU, HE, MS, HS, MH, LH

astm_standards_summary.title: Coal Fly Ash and Natural Pozzolan
astm_standards_summary.classes: Class F, Class C, Class N

blending_ratios.description: Typical SCM replacement levels in concrete mix designs

applications.notes: Ternary blends common (cement + fly ash + silica fume)
Maximum SCM usage to minimize heat of hydration
Per state DOT specifications, low permeability required
High SCM levels or Type V cement for sulfate exposure
Lower SCM levels to maintain early strength for form cycling

carbon_reduction_potential.description: CO2 reduction achievable through SCM use in concrete

reductions_by_scm.notes: Highest reduction per unit of conventional SCMs

reductions_by_scm.scm: Ternary blend (Type IL + 20% fly ash + 10% slag)

industry_targets.pca_roadmap_2050: Carbon neutral cement and concrete by 2050
key_levers: Clinker substitution with SCMs (largest near-term lever)
Carbon capture and storage (CCS) at cement plants

fly_ash.primary_risk: Coal plant retirements eliminating fresh supply

slag_cement.primary_risk: Blast furnace closures as EAF steelmaking grows
mitigation: Imported GBFS from Brazil, Japan, India

natural_pozzolans.primary_risk: Logistics cost from remote deposits to demand centers

calcined_clay.primary_risk: Technology commercialization timeline and specification acceptance
mitigation: Global technology transfer from India/Colombia pilots
